content.problemStatement:
Chefina likes prefix and suffix sums, so Chef decided to give some to her as her birthday present. He created a sequence a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​ and calculated its prefix sums pre1,pre2,…,preNpre_1, pre_2, \ldots, pre_Npre1​,pre2​,…,preN​ (for each valid iii, preipre_iprei​ is the sum of the first iii elements of aaa) and suffix sums suf1,suf2,…,sufNsuf_1, suf_2, \ldots, suf_Nsuf1​,suf2​,…,sufN​ (for each valid iii, sufisuf_isufi​ is the sum of the last iii elements of aaa). He arranged the elements of these sequences in a gift box and went to Chefina's home.

When Chefina opened the gift box, she found out that all the elements got shuffled when Chef was carrying the box. For each element, it is now impossible to determine if it belonged to the sequence preprepre or sufsufsuf and what its index in the correct sequence was. The only thing we know is a sequence x1,x2,…,x2Nx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_{2N}x1​,x2​,…,x2N​, which contains all the elements of the sequences preprepre and sufsufsuf, in no particular order.

Chefina is now curious about the number of initial sequences aaa which Chef could have chosen, such that it is possible to obtain xxx by shuffling the prefix and suffix sums of aaa. Help Chefina find this number. Since it could be very large, compute it modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains 2N2N2N space-separated integers x1,x2,…,x2Nx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_{2N}x1​,x2​,…,x2N​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of possible initial sequences modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.
